ogTitle: South Quairading vs South Stirling: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Quairading and South Stirling.
heading: South Quairading vs South Stirling — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Quairading, Western Australia and South Stirling, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Quairading has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to South Stirling (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Quairading or South Stirling safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Quairading has a flood risk score of 60/100, while South Stirling scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Quairading scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. South Stirling scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Quairading or South Stirling?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Quairading has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
